steps.title: Match the claim to imagery
steps.content: The claim says the speaker is determined to experience the countryside around her. A strong supporting quotation should therefore show both resolve, such as a decision to walk according to her own wishes, and concrete rural imagery rather than only abstract reflection.

steps.title: Select the walking passage
steps.content: Choice A describes returning to earlier feelings and leaving behind the pursuit of wealth and learning, but it does not show the speaker actively choosing the countryside. Choice B lists places or traditions the speaker will not follow, so it focuses on rejection rather than countryside experience. Choice D also describes what the speaker will not seek. Choice C says, I'll walk where my own nature would be leading, which shows determination, and it includes grey flocks, ferny glens, wild wind, and a mountain side, all of which support the countryside claim. The answer is C.
